_README: MmapGen per-map / per-tile generator config. Top-level keys are stringified map IDs; values for that map are flat scalars (agentRadius, agentHeight, walkableRadius, walkableHeight, walkableSlopeAngle, etc.). Per-tile overrides go in nested objects under the map (the inner key is `tileX || tileY`, no separator). The `default` block at the top is read by tools/NavDataAudit only -- MmapGen itself ignores it. See docs/physics/MMAP_FORMAT.md.
_agentNotes: Tauren Male is the largest WoW capsule (radius 1.0247, height 2.625). All continent maps must be generated for that capsule. agentRadius / agentHeight are in WoW meters. walkableRadius/walkableHeight/walkableClimb were previously specified as integer cell counts tied to ch=0.25 / cs=0.2666 — that's a footgun when ch or cs change (PFS-OVERHAUL-006: setting them to 0 lets TileWorker auto-derive cell counts from agentRadius/agentHeight/agentMaxClimbModelTerrainTransition divided by cs/ch, producing correct world-unit erosion / clearance / climb regardless of cell size).

1._4029_README: Per-tile override for OG zeppelin tower tile. CLI invocation is `MmapGen.exe --tile 40,29` (writes mmaps/0012940.mmtile via filename swap). The tile-config key uses the CLI convention `tileX || tileY` = "4029". Earlier `"2940"` key never fired (looked up via tileX=40 tileY=29 → key "4029", not "2940"). PFS-OVERHAUL-006 Cycle 16, 2026-05-08.
1._4029_README_cs: cs=0.1m (vs 0.2666m default) for fine horizontal resolution on thin overhanging structures (the deck edge wall + railing). At cs=0.2666, voxel col=(27,61) at WoW(1337.25,-4645.11) collected three stacked spans at worldY 51.5/52.3/53.5 with only 0.6-0.9y between them; rcFilterWalkableLowHeightSpans then zeroed the deck-floor span (51.5..51.7) because it had less than walkableHeight=2.625y of overhead — leaving no walkable poly at the deck height that the live tests need. Cycle-16 diagnostic confirmed via --debug-heightfield. Tile size grows with cs=0.1, but only for this one tile.
1._4029_README_ledgefilter: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 / Phase 6 per-tile ledge-filter overrides. treatOobNeighborAsCliff=false: dock cells whose voxel-column neighbors are out-of-tile-bounds (OG harbor edge) stop counting OOB as an infinite drop, so the ledge filter doesn't reject them. mixedAreaUsesTerrainClimb=true: dock-edge cells transitioning to model decorations on top (Burningmidtree, Cage03, Stormwindcrate, Darnassusstreetlamp) keep using the 1.8y terrain climb instead of the tighter ~1.2y model-transition climb. Both flags default to legacy behavior in getDefaultConfig() so non-opted tiles are unaffected. See TileWorker.cpp::filterLedgeSpans.
1._4029_README_climb: PFS-OVERHAUL-006 Cycle 17 / Intervention 3 (2026-05-08): per-tile climb override required to disconnect the polygon corridor's lower-platform → upper-deck shortcut at the OG zeppelin tower deck-lip. Combined with the smooth-path Z-delta interpolation in Exports/Navigation/PathFinder.cpp (max-Z-delta=0.5y), this produces a clean spiral-ramp ascent without the bot hitting the 1.84y deck-lip wall. Default walkableClimbTerrain=1.8y is the harvested-from-client baseline; here we tighten to 0.2y for tile (40,29) ONLY. See memory project_pfs_overhaul_006_intra_tile_disconnect.
